mnemonics.title: IPv6 Address Format
mnemonics.content: IPv6 addresses are 128 bits long and are typically written in hexadecimal notation, with each group of four hexadecimal digits separated by a colon.

mnemonics.title: IPv6 Datagram Format
mnemonics.content: The IPv6 datagram format consists of a header and a payload, with the header containing fields such as Version, Traffic Class, Flow Label, Payload Length, Next Header, Hop Limit, Source Address, and Destination Address.

mnemonics.title: Extension Headers
mnemonics.content: Extension headers are used to provide additional information about the packet, such as the Hop-by-Hop Options Header, Destination Options Header, Routing Header, and Fragment Header.

mnemonics.title: Flow Label Field
mnemonics.content: The Flow Label field is used to identify a flow of packets and is typically used for Quality of Service (QoS) purposes.

mnemonics.title: IPv4 vs IPv6 Datagrams
mnemonics.content: IPv6 datagrams are larger than IPv4 datagrams, have a longer header, and use a different address format.
